hook.text: Governments still use waterfall more than any other sector — procurement rules practically require it.

buildup.text: Public bidding requires defined scope, fixed budgets, and contractual milestones. That's waterfall's language.

discovery.text: The U.S. Digital Service and UK's Government Digital Service are pushing agile into government — slowly.

twist.text: Changing procurement law is harder than changing technology. The process, not the technology, keeps waterfall alive.

climax.text: Government waterfall persists not because it works best, but because the system was built around it.

punchline.text: Governments don't use waterfall by choice. The rules leave no alternative.

quiz.question: Why does government still predominantly use waterfall?
options: Procurement rules require defined scope, fixed budgets, and contractual milestones
Agile has been banned by law
